title: Cayratia novemfolia
taxonomy: Cayratia novemfolia belongs to the family Vitaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as grape or vine family. The genus Cayratia is a part of the tribe Viteae, and the species novemfolia is a type of flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. Cayratia novemfolia is a climber with leaves that are usually cordate or ovate in shape and have a distinctive arrangement of veins. The plant is also known as the Nine-leaved grape or the Five-leaf vine in some regions. Its classification places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida.
uses: Cayratia novemfolia, also known as the nine-leaved Cayratia, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Its leaves and stems are used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues, as well as to reduce inflammation. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections. Additionally, Cayratia novemfolia is used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its purported ability to regulate gut health. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cayratia novemfolia as a treatment for these conditions.

side_effects: Cayratia novemfolia is a plant used in traditional medicine, but its use may have potential side effects.
